title: Return workflow status back to workflow API
body:
## Present setup

Today we expose the `/agent/run` in Dapr Agents for initializing an agent workflow and subsequently `/agent/run/<workflow_id>` for the workflow status (it's also here we have `/purge`, `/stop`, etc.).
The status however does not tell much about where in the execution the agent is which means polling the endpoint is either "still running" or "done" with no details in between. For long running agents - and especially orchestration scenarios - this gives a less than ideal experience as the user may wonder whether anything is actually happening.

## Proposed solution

We should bubble out the `last_activity` + relevant metadata to the workflow API that we consume from Dapr Agents API and expose to the `/agent/run/<workflow_id>` endpoint so the user can get a better understanding on where the agent is in its execution flow.

This could also benefit workflows in general as we don't believe this to be an Agent specific thing.
